Name: Multi-dimensional Array View with Strides (Row-Major)
Summary: Implements a lightweight, non-owning view over a flat memory buffer, allowing it to be accessed as a multi-dimensional array using row-major indexing and dynamically computed strides. This pattern is fundamental for building custom numerical libraries, interacting with external data formats, or managing memory efficiently in compute-intensive applications.
Usage: To apply this skill, create a `Tensor` object by providing a raw pointer to your flat data buffer and a `std::vector<int>` representing its desired multi-dimensional shape. You can then access elements using `at({dim0_idx, dim1_idx, ...})`. This pattern can be extended to support different data types (e.g., `double`, `int`), add basic arithmetic operations, or integrate with memory pool allocators for advanced memory management.
author: anonymous
